chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Application No.3069 of 2025 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz ul Hassan Shah Applicant:- Noor Bakhsh through Mr. Sajid Mangi, Advocate. Respondent:- The State through Mr. Ali Haider Salim, Additional Prosecutor General. Date of hearing:- 01.12.2025 O R D E R =============== MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested on 04.06.2025 by a police team of P.S. Sohrab Goth, headed by Sub-Inspector Muhammad Aslam from Baoon Shah Mazar Road, Shahaban Town, near Estate Life Zameen Malir City, Karachi and from him allegedly 250 gram of heroin, 51 grams of ice and 110 grams of crystal were recovered, hence this case against him. 2. Learned counsel for applicant submits that the applicant has been falsely implicated in this case; that there is no video recording of the incident as mandated by section 7 of Sindh Control of Narcotics Substance Act, 2024 and the applicant is first offender and alleged offence does not fall within prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr. P.C. 3. On the other hand, Learned APG has opposed bail. 4. Nonetheless, it is not disputed that the applicant is first offender and offence does not fall within the prohibitory clause of Section 497 Cr. P.C. The investigation is complete and applicant is no more required for further inquiry or investigation. The applicant is in jail for last six months and no progress has been made. Consequently, the bail application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5.

Consequently, the bail application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R. Bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. The Bail Application is disposed of accordingly. The observations herein above are tentative in nature and shall not affect the case of either party on merits before the trial Court. JUDGE HANIF JUDGE
